title: quercus chrysolepis var. hansenii
intro: Quercus chrysolepis var. hansenii, also known as the golden cup oak, is a lesser-known variety of oak tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds found in its leaves and bark. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, and has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. Quercus chrysolepis var. hansenii is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts are also used in herbal supplements, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
